summary: Drew DeVault gives a brief September 2022 status update while sick with COVID-19. He highlights Hare's new cross-compilation support and progress toward TLS, introduces powerctl as a small Hare systems programming project, and describes major refactoring work on the Helios microkernel focused on capability lifecycle management and address space IDs.
key_points: Hare now supports easy cross-compilation, with bigint patches in queue as a step toward TLS support
powerctl is a new small project for Linux power management that serves as a Hare systems programming case study
Helios is undergoing major refactoring to properly handle capability object lifecycles (creation, copying, destruction)
Address space IDs (PCIDs on x86_64) were added to Helios, promising future performance improvements
money_quotes: I have COVID-19 and I am halfway through my stockpile of tissues, so I'm gonna keep this status update brief.
I finally put the last pieces into place to make cross compiling as easy as possible.
I have a bunch of new stuff in my patch queue to review once I'm feeling better, however, including bigint stuff — a big step towards TLS support.
It makes for a good case study on Hare for systems programming.
The key is reworking these features to take better consideration of the full object lifecycle — creating, copying, and destroying capabilities.
key_insight: Even a brief status update shows the breadth of DeVault's systems work, from language toolchain improvements in Hare to microkernel capability redesign in Helios.
